perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information if so required by judicial or administrative proces s, provided that it shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party so as to allow the Disclosing Part y reasonable time to oppose such process, and provided that Receiving Party shall minimize su ch disclosure to the extent possible.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Material if so required by judicial or administrative proces s, provided that it shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party so as to allow the Disclosing Part y reasonable time to oppose such process, and provided that Receiving Party shall minimize su ch disclosure to the extent possible.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Material' in the disclosure clause, there is ambiguity about whether court-mandated disclosures of 'Confidential Material' are subject to the same notification and minimization requirements. This creates a conflict, especially if 'Confidential Material' is interpreted differently from the defined 'Confidential Information.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION . 1.1 Definition. The term "Confidential Information" shall include and mean any and all information, data, product plans, diagrams and know-how programs furnished at any time by the Disclosing Party or by any of its affil iates to the Receiving Party; whether in oral, written, graphic or machine-readable form, includin g but not limited to current or projected components, parts, software, hardware, technical an d other data, research material, inventions, discoveries, designs, drawings, concept s, procedures, uses of materials, ideas, diagrams, marketing plans, brochures, photographs, processes, combinations, test equipment, test data, specifications, operational d ata, models, prototypes, materials, methods and techniques.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Material' introduces ambiguity. The original text clearly defines 'Confidential Information'. The modified text uses 'Confidential Material', which is not defined, potentially excluding some information covered under 'Confidential Information' or including additional information not originally intended. This creates a conflict, as the contradicted text specifically defines 'Confidential Information' and the changed text uses a different term without clarifying its relationship to the defined term. Therefore, it triggers a 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the term is used interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations on whether court-mandated disclosures are subject to notification and minimization requirements.

perturbation.original_text: 3. P ROPRIETARY NATURE . 3.1 Ownership. All Confidential Information is and shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. The parties acknowledge that all Confidential Information is the sole property of the Disclosing Party and that the Recei ving Party shall not acquire any proprietary interest in the Confidential Informatio n. All applicable rights to mask works, topographies, patents, copyrights, trademarks and t rade secrets with respect to the Confidential Information are retained exclusively b y the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. P ROPRIETARY NATURE . 3.1 Ownership. All Private Data is and shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. The parties acknowledge that all Confidential Information is the sole property of the Disclosing Party and that the Recei ving Party shall not acquire any proprietary interest in the Confidential Informatio n. All applicable rights to mask works, topographies, patents, copyrights, trademarks and t rade secrets with respect to the Confidential Information are retained exclusively b y the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 3.1, changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Data' in the first sentence creates a contradiction. The first sentence states that 'Private Data' is the property of the Disclosing Party, while the subsequent sentences refer to 'Confidential Information.' This implies two distinct data types with conflicting ownership claims within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. P ROPRIETARY NATURE . 3.1 Ownership. All Private Data is and shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. The parties acknowledge that all Confidential Information is the sole property of the Disclosing Party and that the Recei ving Party shall not acquire any proprietary interest in the Confidential Informatio n. All applicable rights to mask works, topographies, patents, copyrights, trademarks and t rade secrets with respect to the Confidential Information are retained exclusively b y the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Data' in the first sentence of Section 3.1. Because the subsequent sentences within the same section refer to 'Confidential Information', this creates a conflict and contradiction. The first sentence states that 'Private Data' is owned by the disclosing party. The other sentences in the same section refer to Confidential Information, implying 2 different types of data with conflicting ownership claims, which creates a direct contradiction within the document. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon such termination or other expiration of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will return all of the Confidential Information in written or other tangible form, including any copies made, to the Disclosing Party together with certification that any other copies or notes or summaries (to the extent i ncluding the Confidential Information) have been destroyed. The provisions of Sections 1 t hrough 5 of this Agreement shall survive any termination or expiration.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon such termination or other expiration of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will return all of the Confidential Records in written or other tangible form, including any copies made, to the Disclosing Party together with certification that any other copies or notes or summaries (to the extent i ncluding the Confidential Information) have been destroyed. The provisions of Sections 1 t hrough 5 of this Agreement shall survive any termination or expiration.
perturbation.explanation: By altering 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Records' in the termination clause, it introduces confusion about what needs to be returned upon termination. If 'Confidential Records' and 'Confidential Information' are not the same, the Receiving Party might not return all the information required, leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION . 1.1 Definition. The term "Confidential Information" shall include and mean any and all information, data, product plans, diagrams and know-how programs furnished at any time by the Disclosing Party or by any of its affil iates to the Receiving Party; whether in oral, written, graphic or machine-readable form, includin g but not limited to current or projected components, parts, software, hardware, technical an d other data, research material, inventions, discoveries, designs, drawings, concept s, procedures, uses of materials, ideas, diagrams, marketing plans, brochures, photographs, processes, combinations, test equipment, test data, specifications, operational d ata, models, prototypes, materials, methods and techniques.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the return of 'Confidential Information' upon termination. The changed text alters this to 'Confidential Records'. The 'Contradicted Text' provides a specific and broad definition of 'Confidential Information'. If 'Confidential Records' is not explicitly defined as being equivalent to 'Confidential Information', this creates a strong contradiction. The Receiving Party may interpret the modified text as only needing to return 'Confidential Records', potentially excluding items that fall under the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1, leading to a direct conflict with the original intent of the agreement. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the interchangeable use of 'Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Records' creates conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: this Agreement supersedes all previous unde rstandings or agreements between the parties and incorporates the entire agreement of th e parties with respect to the receipt and use of the Confidential Information; this Agreement may only be amended by a writing of subsequent date that is signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: this Agreement supersedes all previous unde rstandings or agreements between the parties and incorporates the entire agreement of th e parties with respect to the receipt and use of the Protected Information; this Agreement may only be amended by a writing of subsequent date that is signed by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' in the integration clause (Section 6), while the rest of the document primarily uses 'Confidential Information,' a conflict is introduced about the scope of the agreement. Specifically, it introduces ambiguity about whether agreements relating to 'Confidential Information' prior to this agreement are fully replaced, affecting what information is truly covered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION . 1.1 Definition. The term "Confidential Information" shall include and mean any and all information, data, product plans, diagrams and know-how programs furnished at any time by the Disclosing Party or by any of its affil iates to the Receiving Party; whether in oral, written, graphic or machine-readable form, includin g but not limited to current or projected components, parts, software, hardware, technical an d other data, research material, inventions, discoveries, designs, drawings, concept s, procedures, uses of materials, ideas, diagrams, marketing plans, brochures, photographs, processes, combinations, test equipment, test data, specifications, operational d ata, models, prototypes, materials, methods and techniques.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information.' The changed text replaces this with 'Protected Information' in a clause that aims to integrate and supersede previous agreements. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information.' By changing the term to 'Protected Information,' the agreement now introduces uncertainty as to whether prior agreements regarding 'Confidential Information' are superseded. This creates an ambiguity about the scope of the entire agreement and what information is actually covered, thus creating an in-text contradiction as it potentially excludes some of what was originally intended to be included, leading to misaligned terminology.
